name: Downton
coordsSource: web-verified:GENUKI/OS-grid SU182216;Wikidata Q2110657; cross-checked listing-centroid (n=10, gap~0.2km); 2026-06-04
overview: Downton is a large, ancient village about 6 miles south of Salisbury in the Avon Valley, just outside the New Forest National Park, with a population of roughly 3,000. It straddles the River Avon and was historically a market town and a 'rotten borough' that returned two MPs before the 1832 Reform Act, evidenced today by the wide greens and burgage plots of The Borough. The village has a Grade I listed 12th-century parish church (the fourth-largest medieval parish church in Wiltshire) and The Moot, an 18th-century landscaped garden set in the ruins of a Norman earthwork castle.
character: A thriving, self-sufficient riverside village with deep history and a strong community calendar, best known for its annual Cuckoo Fair drawing around 20,000 visitors.
amenities: Co-op food store and village shops
Several pubs including The Bull Hotel (with EV fast charging)
Cafe and village hall with stage
Leisure centre with gym and tennis courts
ATMs at the Esso garage and elsewhere
Industrial estate on the northern edge
Over 20 clubs and societies plus a village band

commutes.notes: Via Salisbury station, then direct South Western Railway

prices.summary: Period cottages along The Borough and riverside homes alongside modern estates; one of the more affordable larger villages south of Salisbury given it has both a primary and secondary school. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: The Moot landscaped garden and Norman castle earthworks
Downton Heritage Trail and water-meadow walks
Walk to Clearbury Ring Iron Age hillfort
Fly fishing on the River Avon
placesToEat: Village pubs in The Borough and High Street
pros: Full village amenities including both primary and secondary schools
Riverside setting on the edge of the New Forest
Strong community life and events (Cuckoo Fair)
More affordable than premium Nadder Valley villages
cons: No railway station; rely on Salisbury for rail
Industrial estate on the village edge
Busy A338 traffic through the area
whoItSuits: Families wanting a large, amenity-complete village with its own secondary school and easy New Forest and Salisbury access.
